What my Puppy Pampers look like with the gorgeous little star model @BaileyandFam 🥹 One thing I forgot to mention is that every Puppy Pamper is completely tailored to the puppy and built up gradually over their monthly sessions. The very first session is often just a bath, brush and blowdry to help them feel safe, relaxed and confident in the salon environment. For puppies that will eventually need haircuts, I’ll slowly introduce little bits such as trimming around the eyes, feet or hygiene areas, but only when I know they’re comfortable and happy with it 🥰 Sometimes it takes a few sessions of just bathing and drying before we move onto anything more. I’ll never push a puppy to do more than they’re ready for and my main goal with the sessions is to make the grooming process as positive and enjoyable for them as possible. I recommend Puppy Pampers as soon as they are covered by their vaccinations 🥰 #goldenretriever #puppy #doggrooming #doggroomer #fyp

bakery style oreo stuffed cookies 🍪🥛    recipe: ingredients: ¾ cup salted butter, softened (170 g) ¾ cup packed brown sugar (150 g) ¼ cup granulated sugar (50 g) 1 large egg  1 tsp vanilla (5 g) 2 cups all-purpose flour (240 g) 1 tbsp cornstarch (8 g) ½ tsp baking soda (3 g) ¼ tsp salt (1.5 g) oreos, some whole some  roughly chopped  white chocolate (measure with your heart)  & choceur cookie and cream chocolate’s   instructions: cream the butter and sugars together  until smooth and slightly fluffy. mix in the egg and vanilla until fully combined. in a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt. fold the dry ingredients into the wet just until combined.  chop some of the oreos into small chunks ( leave some for topping ) and fold them in along with the white chocolate. scoop the dough into 8 portions, flatten and add an oreo inside and chocolate then shape into tall mounds. ( i flatten the top but keep them tall ) bake at 375°F for 11–14 minutes, until the edges are set and the centers are still soft. ( baking time may vary ) let cool to set the cookies!  i added moser roths white chocolate to the bottom & inside !    #oreo #cookies #chocolatechipcookies #Recipe #baking

A standout shoyu ramen spot in Tokyo. Their signature menu is the “Special Shoyu Ramen.” Built on a chicken-based broth blended with multiple soy sauces, the first sip brings out the richness of chicken oil, deep umami from the soy sauce, and a subtle sweetness. As you continue, the flavors finish clean and balanced. The charcoal-grilled chashu adds a gentle smoky aroma that naturally melts into the broth✨ The straight noodles hold the soup well, delivering its flavor all the way through each bite. The chashu comes in a variety of cuts, each prepared differently—from rich, fatty pieces to lighter, more delicate textures—letting you enjoy a contrast of juiciness and mouthfeel. [Ramen Sakurai 麺屋 さくら井] - Location: 2 Chome-15-27 Kamirenjaku, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Hours: Mon & Sat 11:30 - 15:00 / 18:00 - 20:30, Tue/Thu/Fri 11:30 - 15:00, Sun 11:30 - 16:00 (Closed Wed) - Menu: Special Shoyu Ramen (¥2,000) #ramen #tokyoramen #shoyuramen #라멘

What a journey it has been bringing this book to life! I first began writing Silver is the Sky a year and a half ago. I remember that it was December because as I outlined on a giant piece of parchment paper laid out across my floor, my fingers were cold and I was wearing a big fluffy pink sweater. I was so excited for this new adventure. Then...I wrote 'vampires' on a sticky note and applied it to the parchment. I rocked back on my heels, warming my fingers on my mug of peppermint tea, and stared at the word. Vampires. Vampires? You have GOT to be kidding me, God.  Nah. That wasn't God. That was just some weird brain fluke. If I didn't want to write about vampires...I wasn't going to write about vampires. You ever get that weird feeling you just heard God chuckle? Because as I drafted, the theme became clearer and clearer. Vampires crafted after the Nephilim. Human traffic king. Human sacrifice. DNA altering. The intentional corruption of human blood. Lies that sound so close to truth you start to believe them, and only realize you're believing them when these toxic beliefs begin to shape how you see yourself, your world, and God. Alright. So God wanted me to write about vampires.  The thing is, all these myths and legends...they came from somewhere. And when we look at them through the lens of what we know from the Bible, the truth becomes frighteningly clear. "For we do not wrestle against flesh and blood"...we know this part of the verse well. A reminder that our fellow man is not the enemy. But what about the second part of the verse? The part that tells us who we really are at war with? ..."but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places". Too often, we as Christians outsource the handling of these topics to the secular market. We don't want to think about demons. We don't want to consider spiritual causes of our troubles. We don't want to face that we have a real, present enemy who hates us because God loves us, and he hates God.  Make no mistake. Satan hates us. I believe he has made a study of us. It started with blood. And it ends... With blood. Silver is the Sky. 5/29/2026 #christianfantasyromance #christianfantasy #darkchristianfantasy #nospicefantasyromance #christianfantasybooks

Bakery style chocolate chip cookies 🍪  Ingredients  3/4 cup (170g) unsalted butter, cold and cubed  3/4 cup (150g) dark brown sugar 1/3 cup (65g) granulated sugar 1 large egg + 1 egg yolk, room temp 2 tsp vanilla bean paste 2 cups (240g) all-purpose flour 1 tsp cornstarch 1 tsp espresso powder 1/2 tsp baking soda 1 tbsp milk powder  1/4 tsp baking powder 3/4 tsp salt 1 cup semi sweet chocolate chips 3/4 milk chocolate chips  Flaky sea salt for topping Method : In a bowl add all purpose flour, baking soda, baking powder, milk powder, espresso powder , corn starch and salt. Mix well to combine. In a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, add the cold cubed butter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar. Mix on medium speed for about 2–3 minutes, until the mixture looks creamy.  Add the egg, egg yolk, and vanilla bean paste. Mix until fully combined, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture. Mix on low speed just until combined—do not overmix. Stir in the semi-sweet and milk chocolate chips until evenly distributed.  Cover and refrigerate the dough for at least 2 hours ( or up to 24 hours for deeper flavor and thicker cookies). Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Scoop large portions of dough (about ounce each) and roll loosely into balls. For a bakery look, keep them tall rather than flattened. Bake for 15-16 minutes, or until Edges are set and lightly golden. Centers are still soft and slightly underbaked. Sprinkle flaky sea salt on top immediately after baking. Let cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5–10 minutes before transferring to a wire rack. #cookies #cookierecipes #chocolatechip #cookierecipe

This routine is for anyone who is not dealing with extreme damage but wants over all glossier & softer hair this is the the exact routine I built for getting glass hair on fine hair but will work for the wavy curly hair girls too!!  the science: the flatten your cuticle lies, the more light it reflects that's what gives the illusion of glass. glossy hair isn't a product, it's the consistency of routine over time that’s keeping that cuticle smooth from prewash to final styling  it took me a while to figure out the system, but once I did, the results compounded and now I wanna share my favourite tips with you because this is the routine that actually for every  wash day. the 5 things I never skip: 1. always create a barrier before shampoo: hair oil or conditioner first. less friction = less cuticle damage = more shine 2. weekly hair mask. deep hydration is what keeps that cuticle flat and the shine consistent. 3. a glossing treatment every 3-4 weeks  this is the step that takes glossy to glass  4. leave-in conditioner every single wash. beats frizz and keeps the cuticle flat between washes. 5. the hair sandwich method oil before heat styling AND after. then always finish with a cool shot to lock the cuticle in place ✨ Hope these haircare tips help you guys!! #hair #haircare #glasshair #hairgloss

Creamy Cheesy Buldak Ramen 🍜🧀🔥 Ingredients 1 pack Buldak ramen 1 slice baby Swiss cheese 1 slice American cheese 1 slice Monterey Jack cheese 2 tbsp mayo Buldak sauce packet Buldak powder packet Chili oil 2 cloves garlic, minced 2 tbsp chopped green onions ½ cup heavy cream or milk Cooked ramen noodles Toppings Fried egg Mini sausages Dumplings Extra green onions Extra chili oil Instructions Cook the Buldak noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside. In a pan over low heat, add the baby Swiss, American, and Monterey Jack cheese slices. Add the mayo, Buldak sauce, Buldak powder, chili oil, minced garlic, and green onions on top of the cheese. Mix everything together until the cheese melts into a thick spicy paste. Pour in the heavy cream or milk and stir until smooth and creamy. Add the cooked noodles and toss until fully coated in the sauce. Top with fried egg, dumplings, mini sausages, extra green onions, and more chili oil if desired. Enjoy hot and creamy  #buldak #buldakramen #eat #food  #Foodie

In a pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant. Add Kewpie mayo, Buldak sauce, and powder packet. Mix well until it turns thick and glossy. Add chili oil and stir. Pour in heavy cream and mix until smooth, creamy, and slightly thickened (that rich orange color 🔥). Add noodles and toss until fully coated. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese on top and let it melt into the sauce. Add dumplings and sausages on top. Cover and let everything heat through. Finish with green onions, sesame seeds, and extra chili oil drizzle. . #buldak #buldakramen #eat #cook #Foodie

Raising Cane’s-Style Chicken Fingers With Cane’s Sauce. Ingredients: For the Chicken: - 2 lb chicken breast, cut into strips - Oil for frying For the Wet Batter: - 2 cups buttermilk - 2 tbsp garlic powder - 1 tbsp black pepper - Salt to taste - 1 tbsp smoked paprika For the Dry Batter: - 2 cups all-purpose flour - 3 tbsp cornstarch - Salt to taste - 1 tbsp garlic powder - 1 tsp black pepper - 1 tsp smoked paprika For the Cane’s Sauce: - 1 cup mayonnaise - 4 tbsp ketchup - 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce - 1 tbsp garlic powder - 1 tsp black pepper - Salt to taste - 1 tsp smoked paprika Instructions: 1. Prepare the wet batter by whisking together buttermilk, garlic powder, black pepper, salt, and smoked paprika. Add the chicken strips to the wet batter and marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight. 2. Prepare the dry batter by whisking together flour, cornstarch, salt, garlic powder, black pepper, and smoked paprika. 3. Remove the chicken from the wet batter, allowing excess to drip off, then coat each strip in the dry batter. 4. Deep-fry the coated chicken strips in hot oil (about 350°F) until golden brown and cooked through, approximately 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway through. 5. Prepare the Cane’s Sauce by whisking together mayonnaise, ketchup,Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder, black pepper, salt, and smoked paprika. 6. Serve the crispy chicken fingers with Cane’s Sauce. #canessauce #raisingcanes #friedchicken #chickentenders #Recipe

Pt.4 | My fall bakery menu 🍂 Recipe below 👇🏼  Crumbl released their Dubai Chocolate Brownie, but why buy it when you can make an even better one at home? 🤎✨ Ingredients 🟤Brownie Base * 180g caster sugar * 2 eggs * 113g unsalted butter (for browning) * 170g chopped chocolate * 80g plain flour * 3 tbsp cocoa powder * ½ tsp salt 💚Kunafa Filling * 260g kunafa pastry (finely chopped) * 2 tbsp unsalted butter (for toasting kunafa) * 320g pistachio cream 🍫Chocolate Ganache * 120g double cream * 100g milk chocolate (chopped) Instructions 1. Preheat the oven to 170°C and line a baking pan with parchment paper. 2. Brown 113g of unsalted butter in a saucepan until golden, then set aside. 3. Melt the chopped chocolate in the microwave in short bursts until smooth, then stir in the browned butter and let cool slightly. 4. In a separate bowl, whisk the sugar and eggs until light and fluffy. 5. Add the chocolate butter mixture to the eggs and sugar, and whisk until fully combined. 6. Fold in the flour, cocoa powder, and salt until just incorporated. 7. Pour all of the brownie batter into the prepared pan, smooth the top, and bake for 22 minutes. Let cool completely in the pan. 8. In a pan over medium heat, melt 2 tbsp of butter. Add the finely chopped kunafa pastry and cook, stirring often, until golden and crisp. Let cool slightly. 9. Mix the toasted kunafa pastry with the pistachio cream until fully combined, then spread evenly over the cooled brownie. 10. Place the pan in the fridge for about 30 minutes to set. 11. For the ganache, place the double cream and chopped milk chocolate in a microwave-safe bowl. Heat in short bursts, stirring in between, until melted and glossy. 12. Pour the ganache over the pistachio-kunafa layer, spread evenly, and refrigerate, ideally overnight, or at least 1 hour before slicing. Enjoy! 🤎 #baking #dubaichocolate #brownies #Recipe #fall

The ultimate drugstore summer / vacation wash day routine! This routine is designed to protect your hair from the sun, swimming in the pool or ocean (with salt & chlorine protection), and frizz caused by hot humid weather. And if you’re using scalp serums, protection against scalp oxidative stress while maintaining the integrity of your scalp barrier. This routine can be completed in 4-6 steps- you can skip pre-swimming oil treatments if your hair isn’t prone to tangling, and you can stick with just one leave in spray depending on your needs. If needed, feel free to add in a bond repair treatment for damage repair, but if you want to keep your routine as minimal as possible (especially to save space packing), this is a great place to start!
